summary: Comprehensive verification of Pricing page + 32 programmatic /tools/:slug pages + expanded sitemap (44 URLs) + LCP optimisations. 36 pytest cases pass (8 new sitemap-v2 + 15 prior SEO + 13 scheduler regression = 100%). Frontend Playwright validated 3 niche tool pages (Nova/Nova/Kai routing), pricing toggle math (29→24), all data-testids, JSON-LD schemas, canonical URLs (cortexviral.com), invalid-slug redirect, and nav-pricing routing. One minor React JSX warning found (fetchpriority should be camelCase).

ui_bugs.issue: JSX attribute uses lowercase `fetchpriority={priority ? 'high' : 'auto'}` which React rejects as unknown DOM property and emits console warning on every page render: 'Invalid DOM property fetchpriority. Did you mean fetchPriority?'. The attribute still bleeds through to the DOM (browser sees fetchpriority=high on hero img), but the warning pollutes console and may be stripped by future React versions. Fix: rename JSX prop to `fetchPriority` (camelCase) — React 19 supports it natively.

action_items: LOW: Fix CVLogo.jsx — change JSX prop `fetchpriority` -> `fetchPriority` (camelCase). Removes a console warning that fires on every page that renders the logo. Functional effect: none today (DOM attribute already applied), but future-proofing.

INFO: Static /robots.txt (CRA fallback) still ends up serving frontend's public/robots.txt which was indeed updated to cortexviral.com — verified curl shows it. Backend /api/seo/robots.txt also correct and includes BOTH Sitemap: directives as required.
critical_code_review_comments: Programmatic SEO architecture is excellent: TOOLS x NICHES cartesian product means adding 1 niche auto-creates 4 new URLs (sitemap + JSX). No JSX duplication, all content niche-tailored through data.js — strong SEO leverage.
getCombo() correctly validates BOTH tool slug and niche slug exist before rendering; otherwise <Navigate to='/' replace/> — verified with 2 invalid slugs (both redirected cleanly, no crash).
Internal-link section emits 6 unique /tools/ links per page (3 related niches + 3 cross-sell tools) — solid for SEO juice flow and crawler discovery.
Pricing monthly→annual math: $29 * 10 / 12 = 24.17 → Math.round = 24. Tested live, displays $24/mo billed annually. Correct.
Sitemap correctly migrated to production domain https://cortexviral.com for all 44 <loc>; image:image namespace declared on <urlset>; one <image:image> per <url> with logo asset.
robots.txt includes BOTH `Sitemap: https://cortexviral.com/sitemap.xml` and `Sitemap: https://cortexviral.com/api/seo/sitemap.xml` — addresses iteration_4 OPTIONAL feedback.
Homepage canonical points to https://cortexviral.com/ as expected post-deploy. CVSeo SITE constant updated correctly.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_seo_v2.py (NEW — 8 pytest cases covering 44-URL sitemap, prod domain, image:image extension, both Sitemap: directives, all 32 programmatic combos)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all data-testids + redirects + JSON-LD validated; 1 minor React warning unrelated to functionality)

test_credentials: Not required — all routes tested are public marketing pages.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend SEO regression test files: /app/backend/tests/test_seo.py (15 cases, original) + /app/backend/tests/test_seo_v2.py (8 cases, prod-domain + programmatic). Pricing page lives at /pricing with data-testids pricing-tier-{free|pro|scale}, pricing-cta-{free|pro|scale}, pricing-billing-toggle. Programmatic pages live at /tools/{tool-slug}-for-{niche-slug}; getCombo() in /app/frontend/src/pages/programmatic/data.js does slug parsing and redirects unknown slugs to /. Sitemap is now 44 URLs (12 core + 32 programmatic). Note: React emits a console warning about lowercase `fetchpriority` JSX prop in CVLogo.jsx — purely cosmetic.
rca of the issue: Minor React warning RCA: `fetchpriority` is a relatively new HTML attribute. In React 18.x it was not in the known DOM property allowlist (camelCase `fetchPriority` was added later). When JSX uses the lowercase attr name, React warns 'Invalid DOM property fetchpriority. Did you mean fetchPriority?' but still passes the attribute through to the DOM (browsers honor it). Mitigation: use camelCase `fetchPriority` in JSX (React maps it to the lowercase DOM attribute automatically). Raw HTML in public/index.html should keep the lowercase form.
